examples.text: He began boasting of his many talents and demanded the Emperor's crown. Smiling, the Buddha declared he would grant that wish— if Sun Wukong could somersault out of his palm. The Monkey King gladly pranced onto the Buddha’s hand and shot into the air, landing in a far-off region

source.text: The tale of the Monkey King and the Buddha - Ji Hao

examples.text: Europeans brought brandy and gin to Africa and traded it for enslaved people, land, and goods like palm oil and rubber. Spirits became a form of money in these regions. During the Age of Exploration,

source.text: A brief history of alcohol - Rod Phillips

examples.text: In what’s now Mexico, pulque, made from cactus sap, was the drink of choice, while East Africans made banana and palm beer. And in the area that’s now Japan, people made sake from rice. Almost every region of the globe had its own fermented drinks.

source.text: A brief history of alcohol - Rod Phillips
